element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial levels demonstrate foundational mechanic of arranging dominoes to fall with single push
Each domino type introduced gradually; 11 total types with different actions identified by red/yellow patterns
Player controls G.I. Ant carrying dominoes one at a time across interconnected platforms
Player must ensure exit remains reachable after domino cascade; ledges cannot be destroyed, gaps must be bridged
G.I. Ant dies if crushed; dominoes destroyed if one lands on another, causing level failure
Large falls kill G.I. Ant; falling off bottom of screen causes death
Time limit present but not failure condition; timeout triggers hint system via pause menu
Some levels have multiple valid solutions; encourages experimentation
Final level requires completion using dominoes with hidden markings; capstone puzzle

typical_tension_triggers: Time limit counting down (non-fatal but pressuring)
Ensuring exit accessibility while placing dominoes
Risk of crushing or falling to death
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Slow deliberate puzzle pacing with escalating spatial complexity; worlds provide thematic rest points; level 98 designer's hint suggests late-game difficulty spike

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-focused; each domino type introduced individually through levels; safe learning environment for core push-cascade mechanic
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing platform complexity; spatial puzzle requirements intensify; multiple domino types interact in single level; exit accessibility becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex interactions between 11 domino types; level 98 explicitly noted as problematic for designer; level 100 uses hidden domino markings as final capstone
notable_spikes: Transition to World 2 (Aztec) - introduces multi-type domino chains
World 6 (Medieval Castle) - spatial complexity increases significantly
Level 98 - designer documented as unable to solve without 'trickery'; suggests unintended difficulty
Level 100 - hidden markings mechanic requires new visual literacy

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels with single domino type demonstrating one mechanic (e.g., how one domino type responds to contact). Safe introductions establishing domino behavior.
Levels requiring player to ensure exit door remains reachable after cascade completes. Can fail if ledge destroyed, gap unbridged, or dominoes block exit.
Each of 9 worlds contains 11 levels with escalating complexity; world completion rewards packet collection; thematic staging provides mental rest between difficulty spikes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World 1 (Industrial), World 2 (Aztec), World 3 (Space Station), World 4 (Electronic), World 5 (Greek Temple), World 6 (Medieval Castle), World 7 (Meccano), World 8 (Dungeon), World 9 (Japanese Temple)

notable_level_patterns.description: Some levels intentionally allow multiple valid solutions, encouraging player creativity and experimentation beyond single-path optimization.
Level 100 (final level) introduces previously-hidden domino markings as the ultimate test, requiring visual pattern recognition mastery.
